description_long: This object is a rectangular guard stone with a two-part construction: a white marble upper section featuring a rounded top and a gray, weathered stone base. The front face of the marble section is inscribed with text, including a name and date, and is bordered by a shallowly carved decorative frame with subtle star motifs. The marble shows signs of age, with some surface discoloration and minor erosion, especially on the back and sides. The base is rougher, darker, and appears to be made of concrete or granite, with visible pitting and moss or lichen growth. The side profiles reveal a scroll-like detail at the top of the marble section, adding a classical touch to the otherwise utilitarian form. This guard stone is solid, heavy, and designed to serve as a protective or commemorative barrier in an urban or historical setting.
description: A solid, two-part guard stone with a white marble inscribed top and a rough, gray stone base, featuring decorative scrollwork and visible weathering.
description_view_0: The front view displays the inscribed marble face with decorative border and text, set atop a rough, rectangular stone base.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the scroll-like detail at the top of the marble section and the thickness of both marble and base.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the plain, unadorned marble back and the full width of the weathered stone base.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, emphasizing the scroll detail and the layered construction of marble on stone.
attribute_location: Typically found at the edge of urban sidewalks, near historical sites, or at the entrance to parks or cemeteries, often positioned to mark boundaries or protect corners from vehicle encroachment.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on a flat surface, with the base resting flush against pavement or soil.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible discoloration and surface erosion on the marble, especially on the back and sides, likely due to prolonged weather exposure. The base shows roughness, pitting, and possible moss or lichen growth, indicating contact with soil and moisture. Minor chipping may be present at the corners from incidental contact.
attribute_historical_markings: The front marble face bears an inscription with a name, relationship, and date, surrounded by a shallowly carved decorative border and star motifs, all in a serif style typical of late 19th-century memorials.

license_info.creator_display_name: Santa Cruz Museum of Art and History
